mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signal broad economic strength and rising corporate revenues, leading to positive revisions in forward earnings estimates and driving equity prices higher over the following month.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between 3-month changes in PAYEMS and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is near the zero lower bound, an increase of more than 10 basis points over a 3-month period signals the start of a monetary tightening cycle, increasing the equity risk premium and causing negative returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: In more than 50% of triggered instances, the S&P 500 produces positive 60-day forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in both retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates a broad-based contraction in household spending and investment, leading to downward revisions of GDP and earnings forecasts, which negatively impacts equity valuations.
failure_condition: A model using both series' changes does not outperform a model using only the single stronger predictor (in terms of walk-forward Sharpe ratio) over a 10-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating growth in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects robust manufacturing and business investment, which translates directly to higher corporate profits and positive equity market performance over the medium term.
failure_condition: The signal's hit rate (predicting correct direction) for 20-day forward returns falls below 55% for any continuous 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion, a falling unemployment rate (UNRATE) below a natural rate proxy (e.g., 5.5%) signals a tight labor market, leading to wage inflation and prompting pre-emptive monetary tightening by the central bank, which is bearish for equities.
failure_condition: When UNRATE is below 5.5%, 60-day forward returns are not statistically lower than periods when UNRATE is above 5.5% (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation print (CPIAUCSL) showing an acceleration beyond an annualized rate of 3% triggers immediate market repricing of the central bank's reaction function, causing a short-term negative shock to equity markets.
failure_condition: The average 5-day S&P 500 return following a trigger event is not negative and statistically different from the unconditional average return.

mechanism_narrative: Higher-than-expected real GDP growth (GDPC1) serves as a positive signal for the overall health of the economy, boosting investor sentiment and expectations for future corporate earnings growth, leading to positive returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: The beta of 60-day forward S&P 500 returns to quarterly real GDP growth surprises is not significantly positive over the full sample period.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of a low unemployment rate (UNRATE) and accelerating inflation (CPIAUCSL) signals an overheating economy, increasing the probability of aggressive Fed rate hikes. This joint condition is strongly negative for equities as discount rates rise sharply.
failure_condition: The average 60-day forward return when the trigger condition is met is not statistically significantly lower than the average return when only one or neither condition is met.
